jokes.text: Do you like this sweater? Jared Leto gave it to me and I've always been on the fence about it.
jokes.punchline: Casual name-drop of A-list celebrity as if normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - shallow fashion obsession mixed with casual celebrity references. Well-structured with the 'on the fence' understatement.

jokes.text: I like it 'cause Jared Leto gave it to you, and he was my first kiss, but I don't know if I like, like it, like it.
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually reveals intimate celebrity encounter
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis - absurd sexual history treated as mundane. The 'like it, like it' repetition adds comedic rhythm.

jokes.text: I knew this day would come, John! Isolated, impoverished, cut off from all medical services! I only prayed it wouldn't be Alexis.
jokes.punchline: Apocalyptic overreaction with casual cruelty
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - dramatic vocabulary for mundane situations plus casual maternal neglect. Character-specific language patterns.

jokes.text: Oh, it's always just a cold, John, until it's full blown... Typhoid!
jokes.setup: Johnny says it's just a cold
jokes.punchline: Moira escalates to tropical disease
jokes.explanation: Standard Moira hyperbole but effective. The dramatic pause before 'Typhoid' sells it.

jokes.text: Do you remember Valentina? Our chambermaid in little Martinique? We all thought she had just a cold, until it was too late! I'll never shake the mental image of her frothing and flailing in the water taxi!
jokes.setup: Explaining why a cold is dangerous
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of servant's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - casual reference to servant's death with theatrical language. 'Frothing and flailing' is perfectly Moira vocabulary.

jokes.text: This is as far as I go, Alexis. One of us has to stay safe for David.
jokes.punchline: Abandoning her for the other child
jokes.explanation: Perfect selfish parenting logic delivered with maternal authority. Economic and character-specific.

jokes.text: While I admire your courage, uh, when it comes to fashion, I'm just not the best person to ask.
jokes.punchline: Polite way to say his outfit is terrible
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie deadpan diplomacy. 'Admire your courage' is a perfect backhanded compliment.

jokes.text: Okay, there are certain lies I tell myself, and if you're any kind of a friend, you will let me cling to those lies.
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission about self-delusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware comedy. David acknowledging his delusions while demanding Stevie enable them. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Can I help you cling to lies, and not drive you to Elmdale?
jokes.setup: David asks for both emotional support and a ride
jokes.punchline: Stevie tries to negotiate partial friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie response - practical negotiation delivered deadpan. Good callback structure to David's request.

jokes.text: It's like a poncho.
jokes.setup: David going to change his sweater
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutal assessment after he leaves
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective fashion roast. Perfect economy and timing from Stevie.

jokes.text: Bob, you're in the room.
jokes.setup: Bob wants to be a fly on the wall
jokes.punchline: Johnny points out the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple literalism joke. Johnny's exasperated logic vs Bob's metaphor.

jokes.text: I'd kill for a bagel, but I haven't seen one bagel since I got to this town.
jokes.punchline: Sets up entire bagel business plot
jokes.explanation: Important plot setup disguised as casual observation. Gains rewatch value as the bagel plot unfolds.

jokes.text: Ooh, I just get chills thinking that we're sitting right in the middle of the 'Johnny Rose Dream Factory!'
jokes.punchline: Roland's over-the-top enthusiasm for mundane muffin eating
jokes.explanation: Good Roland character moment - his oblivious enthusiasm contrasts with Johnny's actual lack of activity.

jokes.text: Has it gotten worse, or are you just not wearing any makeup?
jokes.punchline: Can't tell illness from lack of cosmetics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - vanity disguised as maternal concern. Perfect character integration and timing.

jokes.text: Though I played one once in a lovely little production of 'Harvey,' but she worked in a mental institute.
jokes.punchline: References playing psychiatric nurse in Harvey (which has no nurses)
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - incorrect theatrical reference delivered with authority. Gains value for theater-savvy viewers.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Alexis, I'm sorry I must leave you, but... mummy's gotta fight for her solo.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing choir solo over child's welfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect selfish parenting with dramatic language. 'Mummy's gotta fight' is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: I was with Roland, we were shopping for Jocelyn. It's a long, frightening story.
jokes.setup: Stevie asks how David was in this store
jokes.punchline: Shopping with Roland described as trauma
jokes.explanation: Good David reaction to small-town life. The understatement of calling it 'frightening' works well.

jokes.text: It's a long, frightening story.
jokes.setup: Stevie asking about David shopping with Roland
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic characterization of a shopping trip
jokes.explanation: David's characteristic dramatic overstatement about mundane experiences

jokes.text: Jewish? I didn't know if I could say it.
jokes.setup: Bob hesitating to identify Johnny's ethnicity
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Jewish' like a forbidden word
jokes.explanation: Good small-town awkwardness. Bob's well-meaning but misguided political correctness is character-specific.

jokes.text: But boy, do you all love your bagels! I mean I do too, and I'm not even uh... Jewish. You can say it, Bob.
jokes.punchline: Double-down on stereotyping plus continued hesitation
jokes.explanation: Continues the awkward ethnic stereotype thread. Johnny's patience makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't know why, it feels like a swear.
jokes.punchline: Bob's inexplicable guilt about the word
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. Bob's misguided sensitivity is both cringe and endearing.

jokes.text: Skanky! You called my store skanky when you were here with the mayor.
jokes.punchline: Store owner immediately brings up the insult
jokes.explanation: Good setup for interview disaster. Wendy's directness creates immediate tension.

jokes.text: We're not over the skanky thing, are we?
jokes.punchline: David's resigned recognition of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good David moment - dry observation of his predicament. Economic delivery.

jokes.text: I can say with complete certainty that I see literally nothing but potential.
jokes.setup: Asked if he still thinks store is skanky
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting everything needs improvement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant diplomatic insult. David's attempt at job interview politeness while being honest.

jokes.text: Is the floor overcrowded? Yeah. Are the mannequins a little too busty? Absolutely. Does it smell like urinal cakes in here? Perhaps.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly devastating critiques delivered pleasantly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. David's inability to filter criticism while trying to help. 'Urinal cakes' is a perfect escalation.

jokes.text: Bob want us to meet. Bob wants us to meet?! Yeah, may I sit? No, no.
jokes.punchline: Johnny's horrified reaction to the language barrier
jokes.explanation: Simple fish-out-of-water comedy. Johnny's panic at unexpected business partner.

jokes.text: Well, that may be, but do you know how much a bagel oven costs? And Ivan here, he's willing to do it for free.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining bagel shop was just theoretical
jokes.punchline: Bob presenting 'solution' to cost problem Johnny never wanted to solve
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Bob's enthusiasm creates problems Johnny never intended

jokes.text: Let's turn this dream... Into green!
jokes.punchline: Terrible business catchphrase delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob moment - cheesy business enthusiasm. The rhyme makes it wonderfully terrible.

jokes.text: You know, in France, they say the looser the fit, the sexier the feel, so...
jokes.setup: Trying to sell Stevie on loose clothing
jokes.punchline: Made-up French fashion wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good David character moment - fake sophistication to justify bad fashion. The pseudo-French wisdom is believably David.

jokes.text: I've never been to France.
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact deflation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deflation. Simple but effective. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: But I have my sister's communion this weekend. Okay, don't do that. Don't do what?
jokes.punchline: David's horror at inappropriate outfit choice
jokes.explanation: Good fashion vs. occasion comedy. David's protective reaction to Stevie's inappropriate choice.

jokes.text: This is gonna be a smash at my husband's trial hearing.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to serious legal trouble
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy one-liner. Treating court appearance like social event. Great economy and surprise.

jokes.text: This is gonna be a smash at my husband's trial hearing. He's got good taste.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of husband's criminal trial as fashion occasion
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy treating criminal trial as normal social event requiring fashion choices

jokes.text: Had I known your spirits were so high, I might not have spent the last 45 minutes of my life running around buying cold medication.
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive martyrdom over unwanted help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira passive-aggression. The specificity of '45 minutes' makes the martyrdom funnier.

jokes.text: When have you ever bought me cold medication?! Exactly, I knew it didn't feel right.
jokes.setup: Alexis pointing out Moira's inconsistent caregiving
jokes.punchline: Moira using this as justification for her neglect rather than shame
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Moira's ability to justify her selfishness as logical consistency

jokes.text: Exactly, I knew it didn't feel right.
jokes.setup: Alexis points out Moira never bought her medicine before
jokes.punchline: Moira agrees maternal care felt wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - admitting maternal instincts feel unnatural. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Oh dear, are we sure this isn't just mono again? What with your symptoms, and your lifestyle?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting mono based on Alexis's sexual activity, in front of boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Moira - inappropriate medical speculation about daughter's sex life in front of boyfriend

jokes.text: Maternal instinct, maybe? No, that's not it. No! Maybe. No!
jokes.punchline: Moira's horror at the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Moira's denial. The repeated 'No!' with uncertainty shows her internal conflict perfectly.

jokes.text: go with something a little more old testament?
jokes.punchline: Bob assumes it's about Jewish stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier ethnic awkwardness. Bob's continued misunderstanding is consistent.

jokes.text: I should probably see a business plan so I don't invest so much in another uh, hollow scheme.
jokes.setup: Bob realizing the bagel shop won't happen
jokes.punchline: Treating imaginary business as a real failed investment
jokes.explanation: Good Bob character consistency - he's learned nothing. Calling it a 'hollow scheme' shows his misunderstanding.

jokes.text: it's the same one we use to celebrate my mom's divorces.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to multiple family breakups
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stevie dark humor. Treating family dysfunction as routine is perfectly her character.

jokes.text: Okay, I'll just drop a little raisin in here to release the bubbles. Blechhh! That's no necessary!
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment. David's prissy reaction to Stevie's casual approach to champagne.

jokes.text: Ooh, my first job was actually a gap kids campaign when I was six. To your first job!
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually mentions child modeling career
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis moment - casual reference to privileged past. The interruption structure works well.

jokes.text: Mostly because she didn't cry as much as the unmannerly son, but also because she was adventurous, carefree, so beautiful. Just like her mother.
jokes.setup: Sweet bedtime story about loving Alexis
jokes.punchline: Insults David and makes it about herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - can't give compliment without insult and self-reference. The maternal moment corrupted by narcissism.

jokes.text: Alexis, you're on my arm. Alexis, seriously. I can't feel it! I can't-Alexis!
jokes.punchline: Moira's physical discomfort ruins the intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy ending the sweet moment. Moira can't sustain maternal behavior even when she wants to.
